briefdescription.para: This is the USB initialization function.

detaileddescription.para:
This example initializes the device for Mass Storage Application. The following configuration is done.
EP0 : CONTROL end point, Bidirectional, Packet size 64 bytes.
EP1 : NON_ISOCHRONOUS, BULK_IN, packet size 512 bytes.
EP2 : NON_ISOCHRONOUS, BULK_OUT, packet size 512 bytes

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
None.
None. This is the USB initialization function.

The following is the configuration.
EP0 : CONTROL end point, Bidirectional, Packet size 64 bytes.
EP1 : NON_ISOCHRONOUS, INTERRUPT_IN, packet size of 16 bytes.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
None.
None. This example initializes the device for Microphone Application. The following configuration is done.
EP0 : CONTROL end point, Bidirectional, Packet size 64 bytes.
EP1 : ISOCHRONOUS, BULK_IN, packet size 1024 bytes.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUsb instance.
None.
None. This example initializes the USB device for USB mouse example. The following configuration is done.
EP0 : CONTROL end point, Bidirectional, Packet size 64 bytes.
EP1 : NON_ISOCHRONOUS, INTERRUPT_IN, packet size of 16 bytes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUsb instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the USB mass storage device application.

detaileddescription.para:
CallBackRef
is the callback reference passed from the interrupt handler, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
IntrStatus
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.
None.
None. This function is the interrupt handler for the USB mass storage device application.

CallBackRef
is the callback reference passed from the interrupt handler, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
IntrStatus
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.
None.
Each interrupt source is disabled upon reception. This is to avoid the repetitive occurrence of the same event. This is done because these event conditions exist for few milliseconds. This function is the interrupt handler for the USB mass storage device application.

CallBackRef
is the callback reference passed from the interrupt handler, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
IntrStatus
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.
None.
None. This function is the interrupt handler for the USB mass storage device application.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from to the function, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the USB End point Zero events.

detaileddescription.para:
CallBackRef
is the callback reference passed from the interrupt. handler, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
EpNum
is the end point number.
IntrStatus
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.
None.
EpNum is not used in this function as the handler is attached specific to end point zero. This parameter is useful when a single handler is used for processing all end point interrupts. This function is the interrupt handler for the USB End point Zero events.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the caller function, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.

simplesect.para: EpNum is not used in this function as the handler is attached specific to end point zero. This parameter is useful when a single handler is used for processing all end point interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the USB End point one events.

detaileddescription.para:
CallBackRef
is the callback reference passed from the interrupt handler, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
EpNum
is the end point number.
IntrStatus
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.
None.
EpNum is not used in this function as the handler is attached specific to end point one. This parameter is useful when a single handler is used for processing all end point interrupts. This function is the interrupt handler for the USB End point one events.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the caller function, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts.

simplesect.para: EpNum is not used in this function as the handler is attached specific to end point one. This parameter is useful when a single handler is used for processing all end point interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This file contains the constants, type definitions, variables and function prototypes used in the usb microphone application.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a hvm 12/20/10 First release 4.02a bss 02/20/12 Modified to include Little Endian and Big Endian descriptors.
